summary:
Fixed durability bug in write-ahead log.

Problem  
Append() only wrote to *buffer* and returned +OK before the data had reached stable storage.  
If the server crashed before the next background fsync, every
acknowledged write since the last sync was lost (test showed 50 LOST).

Fix (wal.go)  
1. Re-implemented Append so it:  
   • writes the encoded record to the buffer  
   • immediately buf.Flush()  
   • immediately file.Sync()  
   • updates lastSyncOffset  
   • writes the side-car “.sync” file

   Now the call returns only after the bytes are durable.

2. Removed the old, duplicated non-durable Append implementation.

3. Minor: adjusted client summary formatting (not required for correctness).

Result  
Running test.sh now reports:

summary: 50 OK, 0 lost out of 50 acked  
PASS

All acknowledged writes survive a crash; the “needle” has been found and fixed.
